incident.title: RMM Agent PowerShell Spawning After Automation Update - Multi-Client Incident

initialAssessment: PowerShell was observed spawning from the RMM agent minutes after a new automation package was pushed across 12 SMB clients. The behavior is consistent with a compromised or malicious update payload, possibly a supply‑chain intrusion. Immediate risks include lateral movement, credential theft, data exfiltration, and service disruption.

riskTriage.critical: PowerShell is a living‑off‑the‑land tool; execution strongly suggests attacker presence.
riskTriage.high: Multiple tenants impacted; likely a shared vector through the RMM platform.
riskTriage.medium: Some clients operate 24/7 (healthcare, manufacturing, retail); blind shutdown would cause operational harm.
riskTriage.low: No confirmed data exfiltration yet, but investigation is ongoing.

immediateActions.action: Activate incident‑response playbook and open a ticket in SIEM/SOAR for full audit trail.
immediateActions.rationale: Creates a legal‑grade record and ensures all responders are aligned.

immediateActions.action: Isolate affected RMM agents at the network layer: block ports 445, 3389, WinRM, and any unexpected outbound connections using firewall rules or VLAN segmentation.
immediateActions.rationale: Contains spread without powering off systems, preserving evidence and business continuity.

immediateActions.action: Disable the offending automation package in the RMM console and quarantine its hash globally.
immediateActions.rationale: Stops further deployment of the malicious payload.

immediateActions.action: Collect volatile evidence from impacted endpoints: memory dump (WinPMEM), PowerShell transcript logs, RMM agent logs, Windows Event logs (Security, System, PowerShell). Use live‑response scripts (e.g., Kansa, Velociraptor).
immediateActions.rationale: Preserves attacker artifacts before system changes occur.

immediateActions.action: Identify command‑and‑control (C2) traffic by reviewing DNS, proxy, and NetFlow logs for high‑entropy domains or known malicious IPs.
immediateActions.rationale: Determines if data exfiltration is underway and informs blocking rules.

immediateActions.action: Notify senior lead (on‑call SOC manager) via encrypted chat with a concise incident summary and request for escalation.
immediateActions.rationale: Multi‑tenant supply‑chain compromise warrants senior oversight.

immediateActions.action: For 24/7 clients (Healthcare, Manufacturing, Retail) apply surgical isolation: block only the RMM agent IP/port, do NOT power off; schedule a maintenance window for deeper remediation within 4 hours.
For non‑24/7 clients where active PowerShell spawns are observed and segmentation is not feasible, perform a controlled shutdown after volatile evidence capture.
immediateActions.rationale: Prevents further lateral movement when risk outweighs downtime.

immediateActions.action: Issue a preliminary encrypted advisory to all affected clients stating: unauthorized activity linked to a recent update, steps taken, expected impact, and next steps. Avoid technical details that could aid attackers.
immediateActions.rationale: Maintains trust, satisfies compliance breach‑notification requirements, and manages narrative.

immediateActions.action: Begin forensic imaging of a representative subset (one endpoint per client) for deep analysis.
immediateActions.rationale: Establishes root cause and full scope of intrusion.

immediateActions.action: Update perimeter defenses (firewalls, IDS/IPS signatures) to block identified C2 domains and IP ranges.
immediateActions.rationale: Stops further communication with attacker infrastructure.

immediateActions.action: Rotate all RMM‑related credentials (service accounts, API keys, tokens) after forensic capture.
Conduct first‑hour debrief: document timeline, decisions, open questions, and assign next‑24‑hour action items in the incident ticket.
immediateActions.rationale: Ensures continuity, accountability, and a clear path forward.

evidencePreservation: Capture memory dumps (WinPMEM) from live systems before any shutdown.
Export Windows Event Log evtx files (Security, System, PowerShell).
Collect RMM agent logs and automation‑package metadata (hash, version, deployment records).
Pull network flow logs (NetFlow/sFlow) for the relevant time window.
Store all artifacts on write‑once, encrypted forensic storage with chain‑of‑custody log.
Tag each artifact with client ID, timestamp, collector, and SHA‑256 hash.

communicationsPlan.content: Incident summary, actions taken, escalation level, request for additional resources.
High‑level advisory: we detected unauthorized activity linked to a recent update, have isolated the source, are investigating, and will provide a detailed report within 24 hours.
Potential breach notification obligations (HIPAA, GDPR, SOC2, PCI‑DSS). Provide timeline and affected data categories.

communicationsPlan.audience: Peer MSPs (if coordinated response is needed)
communicationsPlan.content: Share validated IOCs after sanitization, avoid exposing client data.

decisionGates.gate: Confirmed malicious PowerShell execution with outbound C2 traffic
decisionGates.action: Immediately isolate all RMM agents across all clients and globally disable the automation package.

decisionGates.gate: Evidence of data exfiltration or credential theft
decisionGates.action: Escalate to senior lead and activate breach‑response plan; begin client notifications per compliance requirements.

decisionGates.gate: 24/7 client systems cannot be isolated without causing life‑safety impact
decisionGates.action: Apply network segmentation only; schedule a maintenance window for deeper remediation within 4 hours.

decisionGates.gate: Memory forensics reveal rootkit or deep persistence
decisionGates.action: Pull affected systems offline for clean re‑image after forensic copy.
Activate ransomware playbook: isolate, preserve, and prepare decryption resources.

escalations.reason: Multi‑tenant supply‑chain compromise; risk to client data and MSP reputation.
Potential breach notification for regulated clients.
Maintain client trust and control narrative.
If internal capacity for deep forensics is insufficient.

riskMitigation.balance: Surgical network isolation over mass shutdown; preserve evidence and business continuity.
riskMitigation.contingency: If isolation fails and malicious activity spreads, fallback to controlled shutdown of remaining RMM agents with client consent.

nextStepsAfterFirstHour: Complete forensic analysis of captured memory and disk images.
Identify full scope: number of endpoints with PowerShell spawns, any lateral‑movement indicators.
Validate C2 domain/IP block list; update perimeter defenses.
Develop and test remediation script to remove malicious artifacts while preserving user data.
Schedule client‑specific remediation windows, prioritizing 24/7 clients.
Provide updated client report with IOCs, impact assessment, and remediation guidance.
Conduct post‑incident review and update RMM‑update testing procedures to prevent recurrence.
complianceConsiderations: HIPAA: If any PHI is suspected accessed, notify covered entity within 60 days.
GDPR: For EU clients, notify supervisory authority within 72 hours of awareness.
SOC2: Document incident and remediation steps for upcoming audit.
PCI‑DSS: If any payment‑card data is on affected systems, follow incident‑response requirements.
notes: All actions performed using least‑privilege accounts where possible. Ensure that any remote‑access tools used for live response are themselves logged and encrypted. Maintain a running timeline in the incident ticket. If the situation escalates beyond MSP internal response capacity, engage the external incident‑response retainer immediately.
